summary: Completed testing of 2 critical UX bug fixes for Sr-Renovation.fr devis/factures application. Both bugs have been verified as FIXED: (1) Catalog popup in quote/invoice forms now displays items with proper category colors (TOITURE=blue, FAÇADE=orange, ZINGUERIE=green, SOLS=purple), (2) Diagnostic section properly groups sub-options under their parent category, and backend accepts diagnostic as dict.

test_1_catalog_colors_quote_form.details: Catalog popup in quote form shows colored indicators for each category: TOITURE items have blue circles (#3b82f6), FAÇADE items have orange circles (#f97316), ZINGUERIE items have green circles (#10b981). Items also have colored left borders matching their category.

test_2_catalog_colors_invoice_form.details: Catalog popup in invoice form also displays proper category colors - blue for TOITURE, orange for FAÇADE, green for ZINGUERIE.

test_3_diagnostic_grouping_ui.details: Diagnostic section in quote form: Groups like 'Gouttières' can be expanded to show sub-options (Obstruée, Encrassée, Corrosion/Rouille, etc.). UI shows count of selected items '(2 sélectionnés)' when sub-options are checked.

test_4_catalog_api_colors.details: /api/catalog returns all items with color field populated (not null). All 22 catalog items have colors matching their category: TOITURE=#3b82f6, FAÇADE=#f97316, ZINGUERIE=#10b981, SOLS=#8b5cf6.

test_5_quotes_diagnostic_dict.details: POST /api/quotes accepts diagnostic as plain dict object (not rigid Pydantic model). Successfully created quotes with diagnostic data like {gouttieres_obstruee: true, gouttieres_encrassee: true}.

test_6_quotes_diagnostic_persistence.details: GET /api/quotes/{id} returns diagnostic dict correctly. PUT /api/quotes/{id} can update diagnostic with new selections. All diagnostic fields persisted correctly.

critical_code_review_comments: Code implementation is correct - QuoteForm.js and InvoiceForm.js both use getCatalogItemColor() to derive color from category even if item.color is null.
PDFPreview.js imports DIAGNOSTIC_GROUPS from DiagnosticSection.jsx and uses buildDiagnosticLines() to properly group checked sub-options under parent category.
seed_data_creation: Created test clients and quotes during pytest - cleaned up via /api/cleanup/test-data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both UX bugs have been fixed and verified: (1) Catalog colors now display correctly in both QuoteForm and InvoiceForm catalog popups. The getCatalogItemColor() function in both files derives color from item.category using CATALOG_CATEGORIES mapping even if item.color is null. (2) Diagnostic grouping works correctly - DIAGNOSTIC_GROUPS in DiagnosticSection.jsx defines groups with sub-options (e.g., Gouttières with Obstruée, Encrassée), and PDFPreview uses buildDiagnosticLines() to render grouped format like 'Gouttières : Obstruée, Encrassée'. Backend accepts diagnostic as Optional[dict] instead of rigid Pydantic model.
